status_policy.source_supported: Primary sources or pinned implementation sources support the stated boundary; this is not an implementation-conformance claim.
status_policy.supported_bounded: Executable or formal evidence supports the claim only under the recorded model and bounds.
status_policy.under_test: The hypothesis has explicit falsifiers and partial evidence, but decisive implementation evidence is incomplete.
status_policy.unknown: Candidate retained for falsification without a promotion claim.

hypotheses.title: Proof-neutral semantic plane
hypotheses.claim: The ordered accepted-journal manifest and its deterministic semantic fold define ZRM meaning; RISC Zero receipts, folding accumulators, proof trees, releases, and compression topologies only authenticate that relation.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Makes proof backends replaceable and prevents a proof identity or conditional accumulator from becoming economic or commit authority.
hypotheses.falsifier: A proof-backend or tree-topology change alters semantic bytes for the same exact ordered accepted-journal manifest, or an unresolved receipt constructs a verified postcommit fact.

hypotheses.title: Monotone debit and credit limbs
hypotheses.claim: Recursive accounting should accumulate bounded natural debit and credit limbs per typed dimension and derive a signed net only at a boundary; bounded signed net addition has proof-tree-dependent definedness under cancellation.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Provides strong parenthesization independence while retaining exact nonzero coverage even when the final net cancels to zero.
hypotheses.falsifier: Find a tree whose checked limb fold differs in value or definedness under the Lean assumptions, or show the normative schema proves all recursively composed accounting quantities are nonnegative and never cancel.

hypotheses.title: Context-indexed paths with governed bridges
hypotheses.claim: A transition is an indexed arrow from pre-state and pre-context to post-state and post-context. Ordinary steps preserve context; only a separately governed bridge constructor may change policy, verifier, registry, catalog, cost, or proof-adapter roots.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Allows long recursive chains to cross upgrades without weakening freshness or silently reinterpreting earlier facts.
hypotheses.falsifier: An ordinary step changes a context root, a bridge accepts an ungoverned upgrade, or a pre-bridge sealed fact is accepted under post-bridge meaning.

hypotheses.title: Separate composition algebras
hypotheses.claim: ZRM needs distinct type indices or newtypes and law-specific composers for commutative typed effects, order-sensitive journal traces, context-indexed state paths, exact unique sets, and atomic durability; one undifferentiated root and operation must not silently stand for all five.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Turns order, coverage, endpoint, and durability distinctions into type boundaries instead of review conventions.
hypotheses.falsifier: A type-indexed generic representation safely enforces each operation's distinct laws, or an undifferentiated representation rejects every sort, deduplication, endpoint, phase, and authority substitution.

hypotheses.title: Canonical-order serial refinement
hypotheses.claim: Parallel execution should remain a non-authoritative optimization behind an authenticated preset order: every accepted vector, state-root chain, rejection vector, and ordered journal must equal sequential execution in manifest order.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Permits Block-STM or deterministic OCC backends without making worker timing or scheduler topology semantic.
hypotheses.falsifier: Two worker schedules produce different public outputs or either differs from the sequential oracle under the same authenticated manifest.

hypotheses.title: Complete authenticated read mediation
hypotheses.claim: A sealed snapshot capability that records authenticated point observations and complete range or predicate observations is one high-assurance way to mediate every semantic read. Other mechanisms, such as a verified effect system or instrumented evaluator, must prove equivalent access completeness; unauthenticated host declarations alone are insufficient.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Closes hidden-read and phantom dependencies that nullifier or write-set disjointness cannot detect.
hypotheses.falsifier: A hidden backend read changes acceptance, an inserted range element remains invisible, an old-root proof is accepted as current, two conforming adapters disagree on one logical observation, or a simpler mechanism proves complete mediation with the same substitution resistance.

hypotheses.title: Resolved-receipt and decided-accumulator quarantine
hypotheses.claim: Conditional receipts, running accumulators, decided proofs, and verified postcommit facts require distinct types; only an unconditional resolved receipt or final decider result for the governed statement can enter the fact boundary.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Prevents proof-system empty identities, pending assumptions, or deferred verification from masquerading as valid semantic segments.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any public constructor converts a pending assumption or undecided accumulator into a verified fact, or a proof-system zero becomes a semantic empty segment.

hypotheses.title: Private common logic envelope
hypotheses.claim: A fixed outer logic program can privately verify an inner predicate receipt, exact resource statement, typed registry membership, nonrevocation, and cost class while exposing only an explicit public semantic and cost class plus a hiding logic-set commitment.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Directly targets public program-identity linkability while preserving program governance and bounded admission.
hypotheses.falsifier: Distinguish two inner programs in one declared class from public fields, size, timing, roots, or errors; accept a revoked image; or undercharge any program in the cost bucket.

hypotheses.title: Typed registry roles and views
hypotheses.claim: Policies, verifier images, proof adapters, cost models, and acceleration data should occupy domain-separated typed registry roles, implemented either as independent roots or as type-indexed authenticated views rather than one flat untyped namespace.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Blocks cross-role leaf substitution and makes coupling between independent upgrade cadences explicit without requiring one physical tree layout.
hypotheses.falsifier: A proof valid in one namespace verifies in another, or an independent root upgrade changes unrelated statement meaning.

hypotheses.title: Semantic and proof identity firewall
hypotheses.claim: Economic and replay identity should derive from the exact semantic segment, while audit identity separately binds backend, relation, release, topology, control root, and child proof identities.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Avoids duplicate rewards or replay slots for alternative proof encodings while retaining forensic traceability and release governance.
hypotheses.falsifier: Two proofs for one semantic segment redeem twice, a changed semantic statement reuses an identity, or a release or topology change becomes unauditable.

hypotheses.title: Accumulator-independent authenticated state port
hypotheses.claim: Core semantics should consume sealed logical point, range, exact-update, and history facts rather than backend proof bytes or a single accumulator implementation.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Allows sorted-map, Jellyfish, sparse-tree, and later commitment profiles to refine one logical oracle without changing journal meaning.
hypotheses.falsifier: Two conforming backends disagree on a logical operation, a proof crosses profile IDs, or an exact update proof permits an omitted or extra write.
formal_obligations: proof bytes excluded from semantic identity

hypotheses.title: Sibling state and history commitments
hypotheses.claim: Current machine state and append-only transition history should be sibling commitments advanced atomically; embedding history derived from a journal inside the post-state already committed by that journal creates a dependency cycle.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Adds audit continuity without cyclic semantic commitments or confusing inclusion with finality.
hypotheses.falsifier: Construct an acyclic self-contained journal/post-state/history relation with the same guarantees, or accept two incompatible successor heads under the configured independent anchor.

hypotheses.title: Phase-indexed commitment DAG
hypotheses.claim: SemanticEffects, JournalDraft, ReplayMetadata, AtomicBundle, and AcceptedJournal should be distinct phase-indexed types whose constructors enforce a one-way acyclic binding graph.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Makes forbidden back-edges and precommit-to-postcommit authority substitutions unrepresentable.
hypotheses.falsifier: Construct an accepted object with a back-edge, unbound field, phase substitution, or retry cycle, or show necessary retry semantics require one.

hypotheses.title: Proof-carrying research promotion
hypotheses.claim: A candidate high-assurance claim-promotion design is a policy decision over immutable content-addressed evidence receipts bound to the exact claim, scope, checker, toolchain, artifacts, and result; producer booleans and replay strings are not independent evidence.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Turns the research knowledge base into auditable assurance infrastructure and prevents tool status from laundering authority.
hypotheses.falsifier: A producer assertion promotes a claim, a valid receipt for a different claim or weaker scope is accepted, or identical inputs produce different promotion bytes.

hypotheses.title: Orthogonal assurance vector
hypotheses.claim: Evidence assurance should track authentication, replayability, reproducibility, semantic checking, transparency, and freshness independently rather than collapse them into one confidence or support scalar.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Prevents signed, logged, or reproducible but false claims from being mislabeled semantically proved.
hypotheses.falsifier: One scalar preserves every policy-relevant distinction across an adversarial corpus without false equivalence or loss of ordering information.

hypotheses.title: Four-valued immutable promotion
hypotheses.claim: As an engineering evidence model, independent monotone support and refutation bits can yield INCONCLUSIVE, SUPPORTED, REFUTED, or CONTESTED; staleness remains a separate overlay and policy changes create new views instead of rewriting history. PCA and TUF motivate proof-carrying requests and versioned trust, but do not themselves establish this four-valued semantics.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Preserves disagreement and negative evidence, prevents self-support cycles, and makes descendant invalidation exact.
hypotheses.falsifier: Support plus refutation loses either receipt, a self-support SCC bootstraps without exogenous evidence, or refuting a dependency fails to stale exactly its policy-defined descendants.

hypotheses.title: Reconstructive transactional research event source
hypotheses.claim: The research kernel should atomically commit complete reconstructive semantic events with their authoritative state transition, derive materialized views deterministically, keep immutable artifacts in content-addressed storage, and export JSONL only from committed events. A single transactional database is one implementation; an equivalently atomic event log or outbox may satisfy the same invariant.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Eliminates dual-write crash divergence and provides deterministic replay before adding cryptographic recursion over evidence.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any single event mutation, insertion, deletion, or reorder is accepted; live and replay roots differ; a crash yields a half-event or state/export divergence; or a simpler storage topology satisfies the same atomic replay invariant.

hypotheses.title: Composable private policy receipts
hypotheses.claim: Resource logic can consume a sealed nontransferable policy receipt binding action, subject pseudonym, policy and revocation roots, snapshot, epoch, and replay scope rather than embedding all authorization logic.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Makes credential and policy subsystems replaceable while retaining stateful freshness and explicit authority separation.
hypotheses.falsifier: Substitute action, subject, snapshot, epoch, revocation, or scope; replay across scopes; or treat receipt validity as the capability to act or commit.

hypotheses.title: Governed non-uniform circuit family
hypotheses.claim: A fixed versioned family of transition circuits can support heterogeneous ZRM actions through an authenticated router or selector while sharing one typed context shape; circuit-family upgrades require a new registry root or proved bridge.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Avoids a monolithic universal circuit and keeps transition kinds modular without allowing the prover to select a cheaper relation.
hypotheses.falsifier: A prover selects the wrong action circuit, two circuits interpret context differently, or adding a circuit silently changes old statement meaning.

hypotheses.title: Interval-indexed parallel PCD
hypotheses.claim: Independently proved segments may merge only when their authenticated ordered intervals are exactly adjacent and nonoverlapping; worker completion and proof-tree shape remain nonsemantic.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Supports parallel proof generation while preserving exact ordered coverage and state continuity.
hypotheses.falsifier: Reverse, overlap, gap, or duplicate an interval and still obtain the same accepted root segment, or make result depend on merge topology.

hypotheses.title: Governed I-confluent fast path
hypotheses.claim: Ordered serial refinement remains the default; coordination-free merge is permitted only for a governed semantic profile with a machine-checked invariant-confluence or conservative typed commutativity certificate over complete state and effects.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Captures safe coordination avoidance without confusing invariant preservation with serializability or journal equivalence.
hypotheses.falsifier: Two valid descendants merge to an invalid state or externally different result, or a certificate omits a governed read or effect.

hypotheses.title: Typed payload roots and leakage budgets
hypotheses.claim: Application payload commitments should cross the semantic boundary as independently typed and bounded roots, while availability and retention remain separately authenticated claims. Independently, every privacy-bearing receipt should declare public fields, sizes, timing buckets, roots, errors, and linkable identifiers; FLAFOL motivates the information-flow boundary but does not establish payload availability or retention semantics.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Separates immutable semantic commitment from data availability and makes privacy claims measurable rather than absolute.
hypotheses.falsifier: Cross-class root substitution succeeds, an oversized payload is accepted, deletion is claimed to erase an immutable commitment, or a stable public linkability signal is absent from the budget.

hypotheses.title: Authenticated acceleration inputs
hypotheses.claim: Any acceleration data that substitutes for a canonical derivation should either be recomputed inside the verified relation or authenticated against an exact, governed, versioned input profile.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Retains precomputation performance without converting an optimization into an ungoverned semantic input.
hypotheses.falsifier: An accelerated and canonical implementation disagree on an accepted input, or a duplicate key, stale profile, invalid element, or cross-role root is accepted.

hypotheses.title: Arity-adaptive proof aggregation
hypotheses.claim: Binary folding, multi-folding, and aggregation DAG topology can be selected by benchmark behind one semantic interface, provided exact ordered manifests, arity, profiles, releases, and final decider coverage are bound.
hypotheses.architecture_value: Allows hardware-aware proving without freezing proof topology into protocol semantics.
hypotheses.falsifier: A batch omits, duplicates, or reorders an instance through arity or challenge ambiguity, proof state grows contrary to its declared model, or the benchmark loses to the simpler binary adapter.
formal_obligations: proof topology excluded from semantic identity

non_claims: No hypothesis changes ZRM protocol authority, canonical bytes, conformance level, release status, or production readiness.

Bounded Python models, externally observed non-replayable ESSO runs, and abstract Lean theorems do not establish Rust refinement, elliptic-curve security, proof-system security, consensus, finality, data availability, or durable commit.
No folding scheme is selected. HyperNova, ProtoStar, ProtoGalaxy, CycleFold, and RISC Zero remain replaceable evidence-plane candidates.
Research Kernel rankings are discovery metadata only; this registry applies its own explicit evidence policy.
Generic acceleration-input guidance is an architecture hypothesis, not a claim about any particular implementation.
